message:
fix(tests): fix 40+ failing tests in claude_code, opencode, and search modules

- claude_code: add make_test_claude_dir() helper that creates projects/
  marker subdir so looks_like_root() returns true and connector scans
  only the temp dir instead of falling back to real ~/.claude/projects
- opencode: update tests to require both session AND message subdirs,
  matching current looks_like_opencode_storage() implementation
- search: update is_tool_invocation_noise tests to reflect that
  [Tool: Name] entries are now kept (only empty names are noise)
- Include concurrent agent changes (drag-drop, split handle, panel ratio)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.1","title":"Task: Inline mode for cass TUI (anchored UI + scrollback-preserving logs)","description":"## Goal
Add a first-class inline mode powered by ftui:
- UI chrome stays anchored (top or bottom) while logs/output scroll normally.
- Perfect for long-running indexing/sync where you want live status without losing scrollback.

## CLI/Config
- Add flags (names TBD, but must be explicit):
  - `cass tui --inline`
  - `--ui-height <rows>`
  - optional `--anchor top|bottom`

## Implementation Notes
- Use `ftui_runtime::ScreenMode::Inline { ui_height }` + `UiAnchor`.
- Integrate with logging story from `coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.7`:
  - logs scroll, UI remains stable
  - avoid interleaving output from non-UI writers

## Tests
- PTY e2e test:
  - run `cass tui --inline --once` while emitting synthetic logs
  - verify scrollback content remains (no alt-screen)
  - verify UI frame shows at the anchored region

## Acceptance Criteria
- Inline mode never trashes scrollback.
- Inline mode still supports core interactions (search, filters) without flicker.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:31:30.424616284Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T00:31:58.107202985Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","inline","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:44:16.796760464Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:44:17.089206606Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:44:17.379129955Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:31:30.424616284Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Use ftui widgets (tree/table/paginator) to add navigation modes beyond the current list:
- Toggle between:
  - message-hit list (current)
  - conversation-grouped list (collapse multiple hits per session)
- Timeline navigation:
  - jump by day/week
  - quick-jump to most recent activity per agent/workspace
- Quick switchers:
  - agent switcher
  - workspace switcher

## Notes
- Must remain fast; prefer virtualization.
- Grouped view should not change underlying search correctness; it is purely a presentation aggregation.

## Tests
- Unit tests for grouping logic.
- Snapshot tests for grouped view.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Grouped view improves scanability on broad queries.
- Users can navigate to “what I did last week in project X” in a few keystrokes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:36:54.040790465Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T05:53:17.347626392Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T05:53:17.347604511Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.10","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:44:21.978393137Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.10","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:44:21.737368842Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.10","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:36:54.040790465Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.11","title":"Task: Undo/redo for query + filter editing (command history panel, safe state rollback)","description":"## Goal
Add undo/redo for the most common state mutations:
- query edits
- filter add/remove/edit
- saved view load/apply

## Why
A power-user TUI should be fearless: users should be able to explore without fear of losing a good state.

## Implementation Notes
- Leverage `ftui_runtime::undo` and/or `ftui-widgets::history_panel`.
- Define an explicit command set for reversible operations.

## Tests
- Unit tests for undo stack behavior.
- Simulation tests: apply filters, undo, ensure search query + results return to prior state.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Undo is deterministic and never corrupts persisted state.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:49:09.598823035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T06:03:37.952787324Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T06:03:37.952764742Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.11","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:49:09.598823035Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.14","title":"Task: Animation + Visual FX infrastructure on ftui (spring physics, stagger, backdrop blur, gradients)","description":"## Why
The current TUI has basic animations (STAGGER_DELAY_MS=30ms, ITEM_FADE_MS=120ms, MAX_ANIMATED_ITEMS=15, focus flash 220ms color lerp, disableable via CASS_DISABLE_ANIMATIONS=1). ftui provides a physics-based animation system (springs, timelines, stagger, presets) plus visual effects (backdrop dim/blur, text effects, gradients, alpha compositing) that can make cass visually stunning while remaining tasteful.

## What (Merged from former Phase 4A + 4E)

### Spring Animation Migration
1. **Staggered reveal**: Replace linear delay with animation::stagger + spring config. Items spring-in from slightly offset position
2. **Focus flash**: Replace hard timer with SpringConfig (mass/stiffness/damping) for natural settle
3. **Panel resize spring**: Animate layout ratio changes when switching focus (currently instant 70/30→50/50 jump)
4. **Filter pill entrance/exit**: Spring slide animation on add/remove
5. **Modal open/close**: Scale+fade spring
6. **Search-in-progress pulse**: Glow on search bar during async search
7. **Smooth scroll interpolation**: Detail pane scrolls smoothly instead of jumping

### Visual Effects
8. **Modal backdrop dim/blur**: Background content dims to 40% opacity with subtle blur (visual_fx)
9. **Header gradient**: Subtle horizontal color gradient on title/search area (RGBA compositing)
10. **Loading text effect**: Animated gradient sweep across 'Indexing...' text
11. **Agent-colored glow**: Selected result gets subtle glow in agent's accent color
12. **Smooth fade transitions**: Cross-fade when switching between views

## Implementation
- Use ftui-core animation system: SpringConfig, Timeline, stagger, presets
- Use ftui-extras visual_fx: BackdropEffect, DimConfig, BlurConfig
- Use ftui-extras text_effects: gradient, fade, typewriter
- All effects auto-disable on slow terminals via degradation system (SimpleBorders → no effects)
- Respect CASS_DISABLE_ANIMATIONS=1 env var

## Performance Budget
- Animation frame time: <2ms per spring tick
- Visual effects rendering: <4ms per frame
- Auto-disable at degradation level >= SimpleBorders

## Tests
- Unit: Spring physics converges to target within N frames
- Unit: CASS_DISABLE_ANIMATIONS=1 disables all animation
- Snapshot: Backdrop dim renders correctly at each degradation level
- Benchmark: Animation frame time stays under 2ms budget

## Acceptance Criteria
- All existing animations preserved (stagger, focus flash)
- New animations feel natural (spring physics, not linear)
- Visual effects tasteful and non-distracting
- Zero visual regressions on terminals without truecolor
- Performance budgets met

## Depends On
- 2noh9.2.3 (Program skeleton running)
- 2noh9.3.1 (layout foundation for resize animations)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:59:01.996084681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T07:11:58.714497812Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","performance","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.14","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:00:35.769791277Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.14","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:00:39.306356259Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.14","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:59:01.996084681Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.15","title":"Task: Charts + data visualization on ftui (heatmap, bar charts, sparklines, analytics views)","description":"## Why
cass has rich data (session counts, token usage, agent activity) but no way to visualize it in the TUI. ftui-extras provides charts (bar, line, area, scatter) and canvas (pixel-level drawing) modules that can bring data alive in the terminal — a unique differentiator for a coding agent search tool.

## What
Leverage ftui-extras/charts and canvas for terminal data visualization:

1. **Session activity heatmap**: Calendar-style heatmap showing coding session frequency per day (like GitHub contribution graph, but in the terminal). Accessible via command palette or dedicated Analytics tab.
2. **Agent usage bar chart**: Visual breakdown of sessions by agent (Claude vs Codex vs Gemini etc.) — horizontal bars with agent accent colors.
3. **Token usage sparkline in detail view**: Show token consumption over the conversation using Sparkline with gradient colors (green=low, yellow=medium, red=high). Benefits from z9fse analytics data when available.
4. **Search frequency timeline**: Mini chart in footer showing queries per minute (useful for debugging search performance).
5. **Indexing performance chart**: Throughput over time during reindexing.

## Implementation
- Use ftui-extras charts module: BarChart, LineChart, Sparkline
- Use ftui-extras canvas module: pixel-level drawing primitives for heatmap cells
- Data sources: daily_stats table (already computed by analytics pipeline), real-time counters for search/indexing
- Charts render as normal ftui widgets composable in any layout area
- Responsive: charts adapt to available space (min 40 cols for bar chart, 20 cols for sparkline)

## Tests
- Unit: Chart rendering with known data produces expected cells
- Snapshot: Heatmap golden test with mock data
- Unit: Sparkline handles edge cases (empty data, single point, overflow)
- Integration: Charts display real data from daily_stats

## Acceptance Criteria
- At least 3 visualizations implemented
- Charts readable on 80x24 terminal
- Agent colors consistent with theme system
- Degrades gracefully: text-only summary at EssentialOnly level

## Depends On
- 2noh9.3.1 (layout foundation)
- Benefits from z9fse analytics data (not blocking — can use mock data initially)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:59:26.039204969Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T04:56:09.969492012Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T04:56:09.969460053Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","ftui","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.15","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:00:44.689062391Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.15","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:59:26.039204969Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.16","title":"Task: JSON viewer for raw message inspection on ftui (collapsible tree, syntax highlighting, copy)","description":"## Why
The TUI has a Raw tab in the detail pane, but it's just unformatted text. A proper JSON viewer is invaluable for debugging connector issues, understanding agent-specific message formats, and inspecting token usage data (extra_json/extra_bin fields). No other TUI search tool offers this.

## What
Add ftui_widgets::json_view::JsonView for inspecting raw JSON data of search results and messages:
1. **Raw message JSON** with syntax-highlighted keys/values/strings/numbers/booleans
2. **Collapsible nested objects and arrays** (click or Enter to expand/collapse)
3. **Copy-to-clipboard** for individual values (via OSC52 clipboard integration)
4. **Search within JSON** content (Ctrl+F in JSON view)
5. **Path display**: Shows JSON path (e.g., $.messages[0].usage.input_tokens) for selected node

## Implementation
- Use ftui_widgets::json_view::JsonView (tree-based collapsible JSON display)
- Wire as CassMsg::ToggleJsonView — toggle with J key in detail view or command palette
- Render conditionally in the detail pane area (replaces Raw tab content when active)
- Parse message extra_json/extra_bin into serde_json::Value for display
- Syntax colors: keys=blue, strings=green, numbers=yellow, booleans=cyan, null=gray

## Tests
- Unit: JSON parsing handles all serde_json::Value variants
- Snapshot: Render known JSON structure, verify layout and colors
- Unit: Collapse/expand toggle works correctly
- E2E: Navigate to result → press J → see JSON → press J → back to normal

## Acceptance Criteria
- Toggle between normal detail view and JSON view with single keypress
- All JSON types rendered with appropriate syntax colors
- Collapse/expand for nested structures
- Copy individual values to clipboard
- Works with all 14 connector message formats

## Depends On
- 2noh9.3.5 (detail view must exist first)
- 2noh9.3.17 (clipboard integration for copy)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:59:46.838536789Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T05:08:47.574312066Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T05:08:47.574285817Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.16","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:00:56.720525464Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.16","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:00:50.918787212Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.16","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:59:46.838536789Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.17","title":"Task: HTML/SVG TUI screenshot export on ftui (capture current view as shareable file)","description":"## Why
Users want to capture beautiful screenshots of their cass TUI for sharing, documentation, or bug reports without external tools. This is different from export-html (which exports conversation content) — this exports the actual TUI visual state. ftui-extras/export renders Buffer snapshots as self-contained HTML or SVG.

## What
Leverage ftui-extras/export module for TUI-to-file export:

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18","title":"Epic: FrankenTUI analytics dashboard (tokens, cost, tools, plans, projects)","description":"## Why
Token/usage analytics is the missing “quantified self” layer for cass: we already index the entire agent work history, and we already compute token usage/cost signals in SQLite, but there is no best-in-class terminal experience for exploring it.

This epic turns cass into the world’s most functional analytics TUI for coding-agent work by leveraging FrankenTUI (ftui) strengths: deterministic rendering, composable widgets, virtualization, charts/canvas, inspector overlays, and recording/replay for reproducible bug reports.

## User Stories (What This Must Enable)
- “How many tokens did I burn today / this week / this month?” (hour/day/week/month)
- “Which projects/workspaces are costing me the most?” (top-N workspaces; trends)
- “Which agents/models are the biggest spend?” (Codex vs Claude vs Gemini; model breakdown)
- “What is my burn rate right now?” (tokens/hour live + trailing averages)
- “How tool-heavy am I?” (tool calls per 1k tokens; per-tool breakdown)
- “How much planning am I doing?” (plan messages, plan token share)
- “Where is the data real vs estimated?” (API coverage, quality/coverage heatmaps)

## Data Sources (Already In Cass)
- SQLite analytics tables:
  - message-level fact tables: token_usage (v10), message_metrics (v11)
  - rollups: token_daily_stats (v10), usage_hourly + usage_daily (v11)
  - pricing seeds: model_pricing (v10)
- Connectors already extract model/tool/token metadata into NormalizedMessage.extra; analytics ingest reads it.

## IA / Views (Initial Target Set)
1. Overview dashboard: KPI tiles + mini sparklines (today, 7d, 30d)
2. Time-series explorer: interactive chart (metric + group-by + overlays)
3. Calendar heatmap: daily tokens or sessions with color legend + drilldown
4. Breakdowns: tables with sparklines for agents/workspaces/models (sortable, virtualized)
5. Tools view: per-tool usage (calls, avg tokens/call, cost/call)
6. Plans view: plan frequency + plan token share + drift over time
7. Coverage view: where we have API tokens vs estimates, by agent/model/source
8. Drilldown: selecting any bucket opens a filtered search/results view pre-scoped to that slice

## Interaction Model
- Command palette entry: “Analytics” (and sub-commands)
- Global filters: time range, agent, workspace, source, role, metric
- Drilldown is first-class: Enter on any chart/table row navigates to details
- Mouse support where it helps (hover tooltip on charts, scroll)
- Consistent key hints + discoverability via ftui hint registry

## Performance Principles
- Prefer rollup tables for all interactive views (O(#buckets) queries).
- Avoid full message scans; allow explicit “deep analysis” mode when necessary.
- Cache query results per (filters, metric, group-by) with fast invalidation on index refresh.
- Virtualize long lists/tables; render only visible rows.

## Testing & Logging Mandate
- Deterministic rendering snapshots via ftui harness (goldens).
- PTY e2e tests: drive the analytics UI, assert stable buffers, log frame timings.
- Every view emits structured debug logs when enabled (query timing, rows read, cache hits).

## Dependencies (Not Enforced At Epic Level)
Implementation tasks under this epic will depend on:
- ftui TUI foundation (2noh9.*)
- analytics CLI/query surface (z9fse.3)
- model_name + cost support (z9fse.11, z9fse.10)
- per-tool breakdown (z9fse.6)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:10:32.201778623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T08:10:32.201778623Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","ftui","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:10:32.201778623Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18.1","title":"Task: Spec analytics dashboard IA + interactions (views, filters, drilldown, keymap)","description":"## Goal
Write the definitive interaction spec for the ftui analytics dashboards so implementation is cohesive, fast, and consistent with cass power-user workflows.

This spec must be self-contained: future implementers should not need to re-read earlier plans to understand what the analytics UI is supposed to do.

## Inputs / Constraints
- Data is already stored in SQLite rollups (usage_hourly/usage_daily/token_daily_stats) and fact tables (message_metrics/token_usage).
- Prefer rollup-driven UI queries (O(#buckets)), avoid scanning message_metrics except in explicit “deep analysis” flows.
- All time semantics are UTC unless the UI explicitly supports per-user local time zones (out of scope for v1; document that we’re UTC).
- Must be navigable entirely by keyboard; mouse is optional enhancement.

## Information Architecture (Proposed)
Top-level: add an “Analytics” surface accessible via:
- Command palette: `Analytics: Dashboard`, `Analytics: Explorer`, `Analytics: Breakdowns`, `Analytics: Tools`, `Analytics: Cost`, `Analytics: Coverage`.
- Optional hotkey: `g a` (go-to analytics) or `Alt+A`.

Inside Analytics:
1. **Dashboard** (default)
   - KPI tiles: tokens today / 7d / 30d, cost today / 30d (if available), burn rate tokens/hr, tool calls per 1k tokens, plan token share.
   - Mini sparklines per KPI.
   - “Top movers” (agents/projects) with delta vs prior period.

2. **Explorer** (time-series)
   - Primary chart area (line/area/stacked).
   - Controls: metric selector, group-by selector, overlay selector.
   - Overlay options: by agent, by workspace, by source, by model.
   - Cursor crosshair + tooltip.

3. **Heatmap** (calendar)
   - Calendar grid with color scale.
   - Toggle between session count, api tokens, cost.
   - Drilldown from a day -> Explorer pre-filtered.

4. **Breakdowns** (tables)
   - Virtualized table with sortable columns.
   - Per-row sparkline (trend over range).
   - Dimensions: agent, workspace, source, model.

5. **Tools**
   - Per-tool breakdown: calls, avg tokens/call, avg cost/call, trend.
   - Ability to filter to a tool and jump to Search view for sessions that used it.

6. **Cost/Models**
   - Models list with stacked token breakdown + USD estimate.
   - Provider-level aggregation.

7. **Coverage**
   - Coverage heatmap: % API token data vs estimated by (agent x time).
   - Highlight sources/connectors with weak coverage.

## Global Filters (Persisted While In Analytics)
- Time range: presets (today, 7d, 30d, 90d, YTD, all) + custom since/until.
- Agent filter: multi-select.
- Workspace filter: multi-select with search.
- Source filter: local/remote/source_id.
- Role filter (optional for advanced).

These should be displayed in a “filter ribbon” and mirrored in a compact “filters drawer” for small terminals.

## Drilldown Semantics (Critical)
- Enter on any bucket (chart point / heatmap cell) -> opens the main Search results view pre-scoped:
  - since/until set to bucket range
  - agent/workspace/source filters inherited
  - query empty (user can type)

- Enter on any breakdown row -> same, plus an additional filter (e.g., agent=codex).

## Keymap (Draft)
- `Tab` / `Shift+Tab`: cycle analytics subviews
- `g`: open “go to” palette within analytics
- `/`: open filter/search within current view (e.g., table search)
- `Enter`: drilldown
- `Esc`: back (pop view stack)
- `?`: help overlay (view-specific key hints)

## Visual Style (ftui-first)
- Strong typography hierarchy: big KPIs, small labels, consistent number formatting.
- Color scales:
  - tokens: cool -> warm (blue/green -> yellow -> red)
  - cost: green -> amber -> red
  - coverage: red (bad) -> green (good)
- Always include legends for heatmaps/charts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A concrete, implementable spec describing:
  - views, layouts, and minimum terminal sizes
  - filters and drilldown behaviors
  - keymap + command palette entries
  - data semantics (UTC; week/month definitions)
- Explicitly calls out which UI surfaces depend on which analytics beads:
  - z9fse.12 query library
  - z9fse.6 tools breakdown
  - z9fse.10 cost estimation
  - z9fse.11 model_name

## Testing Notes
- Define snapshot test targets (which screens, which fixtures) so engineers know what to lock down.
- Define PTY e2e paths (launch -> open analytics -> switch view -> drilldown -> exit).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:50.160059329Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T20:31:38.608901310Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T20:31:38.608869390Z","close_reason":"Completed: analytics dashboard IA + interactions spec added to docs/tui_keymap_rfc.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","design","ftui","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:50.160059329Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18.10","title":"Task: Analytics drilldown -> Search (push filters + time range + dim; preserve back stack)","description":"## Goal
Make analytics actionable by enabling drilldown into the core cass search experience.

If a chart shows a spike, the user must be able to jump straight to the sessions/messages that caused it.

## Behavior
- Enter on any analytics selection (bucket, row, tool, model) should:
  1. push a new view on the navigation stack (Search view)
  2. pre-fill time filters (since/until) to the selected bucket
  3. apply inherited global filters (agent/workspace/source)
  4. apply the selection’s dimension filter (e.g., agent=codex or workspace=/foo)
  5. leave query empty by default (user types next)

- Esc from Search returns back to Analytics with prior selection preserved.

## Implementation Notes
- Reuse the ftui filter state system (2noh9.3.3); do not create a second filter stack.
- Ensure drilldown uses UTC boundaries consistent with analytics bucketing semantics.
- Avoid triggering double-searches on navigation (debounce or batch state changes).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Drilldown works for:
  - Explorer bucket -> Search
  - Heatmap day -> Search
  - Breakdowns row -> Search
  - Tools row -> Search (when tool filter is supported)
  - Cost/model row -> Search (model filter)
- Back navigation preserves analytics selection and scroll.

## Testing & Logging
- Unit: drilldown state transition produces correct filter state.
- PTY e2e: open analytics -> drilldown -> verify search view is filtered -> back.
- Logging: record drilldown payload (bucket + dim) in debug logs.","notes":"BrownEagle: fixed drilldown filter inheritance in src/ui/app.rs (search filters now reset from analytics filters to prevent stale leakage; selected agent dimension overrides inherited agent set). -{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18.11","title":"Task: Analytics UI test suite (ftui snapshots + PTY e2e + perf logging)","description":"## Goal
Lock down end-to-end correctness of the full FrankenTUI analytics suite and prevent regressions as views, interactions, and query semantics evolve.

Analytics is especially susceptible to subtle defects (bucket boundaries, coverage math, drilldown scoping, stale caches, layout breakage). This task is the final confidence gate.

## Required Coverage Surface
The suite must cover **all analytics subviews**:
- dashboard
- explorer
- heatmap
- breakdowns
- tools
- cost/models
- coverage
- plans

## Test Layers
1. **Snapshot/golden tests (ftui-harness)**
   - each analytics subview at 80x24 and 120x40
   - representative states: normal, sparse, empty, and loading/error
   - deterministic fixtures for stable diffs

2. **Behavioral unit tests**
   - navigation stack and focus transitions across subviews
   - filter persistence and inheritance
   - deterministic sorting/ranking/tie-break behavior
   - drilldown payload construction correctness

3. **PTY e2e tests**
   - launch `cass tui --once` in headless PTY harness
   - navigate across all analytics subviews
   - execute drilldown to Search from multiple subviews and return
   - assert exit code 0, no panic output, and expected rendered states

4. **Performance guard assertions (non-flaky)**
   - capture query/render timings per view
   - enforce broad safety thresholds in CI (catch catastrophic regressions)

## Logging Requirements
On any failure, emit high-signal diagnostics:
- rendered buffer artifacts and diffs
- selected filters/time-range/context
- query timing + cache-hit trace
- drilldown payload and navigation trace when relevant

## Dependency Policy

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18.12","title":"Task: Analytics plans view (plan frequency + plan token share + trends + drilldown)","description":"## Objective
Deliver a first-class **Plans Analytics** subview in FrankenTUI that makes planning behavior measurable, explorable, and operationally useful.

This view should answer:
- How often do plans occur?
- How many tokens are plan-attributed over time?
- Which agents/projects/workspaces are most plan-heavy?
- Where are the exact sessions/messages behind a plan spike?

## Scope
1. Add `Plans` subview to Analytics router with command-palette access and keyboard navigation parity.
2. Render plan-centric metrics using analytics query library outputs:
   - plan message count and share

   - trend lines by hour/day/week/month
3. Provide ranked breakdown tables:
   - by agent
   - by workspace/project
   - by source
   - optional by model (when available)
4. Show quality/coverage diagnostics in-view:
   - detection confidence/coverage notes
   - heuristic version indicator
   - warning states for low-confidence windows
5. Enable drilldown from any plan metric bucket/row to filtered Search with back-stack preservation.

## UX + Interaction Requirements
- Works at both compact and wide terminal sizes.
- Supports fast time-range switching and inherited global filters.
- Uses clear visual cues to distinguish absolute totals vs percentage shares.

  - deterministic table sorting/tie-breaks
- PTY e2e tests:
  - navigate to Plans view

  - drilldown to Search and return
- Failure logs must include rendered buffers, selected filters, and query timing traces.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Analytics router includes a stable, keyboard-accessible Plans subview.

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18.2","title":"Task: Analytics TUI plumbing (view router, global filters, command palette entries)","description":"## Goal
Add the foundational ftui wiring so “Analytics” becomes a first-class surface in the cass TUI:
- view routing / navigation stack
- global analytics filters (time range, agent, workspace, source)
- command palette entries and consistent key hints

This task is intentionally *not* about building every chart; it’s about making analytics views composable and easy to add.

## Requirements
1. **View router integration**
   - Add an Analytics view variant to the top-level app model.
   - Support subviews (Dashboard / Explorer / Heatmap / Breakdowns / Tools / Cost / Coverage).
   - Back-stack semantics: `Esc` pops view stack; drilldowns push.

2. **Global analytics filter state**
   - Persist within the analytics surface.
   - Filter ribbon (compact pills) at top of analytics views.
   - Filters must be serializable so we can later persist them in `tui_state.json`.

3. **Command palette integration**
   - Add commands:
     - `Analytics: Dashboard`
     - `Analytics: Explorer`
     - `Analytics: Heatmap`
     - `Analytics: Breakdowns`
     - `Analytics: Tools`
     - `Analytics: Cost`
     - `Analytics: Coverage`
   - Add `Analytics: Set time range...`, `Analytics: Filter agent...`, etc.

4. **Key hints + discoverability**
   - Use the ftui hint registry so each analytics subview can expose its own key hints.
   - Common keys: Tab/Shift+Tab, Enter (drilldown), / (search within view), g (go-to).

5. **Headless discipline**
   - Analytics views must render deterministically in headless mode (TUI_HEADLESS=1), so we can test them with snapshots/PTY.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The TUI can enter and exit Analytics surface reliably.
- Analytics filter ribbon appears and updates state when changed.
- Palette entries navigate to each analytics subview (even if some are placeholders initially).
- No panics on small terminals; show graceful “window too small” message.

## Testing & Logging
- Unit: routing state machine (enter analytics, switch view, back stack).
- Snapshot: base analytics shell renders deterministically (no data).
- Logging: trace view transitions and filter changes behind a debug flag.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:14:16.904103570Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T02:24:31.566677556Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T02:24:31.566649774Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","architecture","ftui","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:14:16.904103570Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:14:16.904103570Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:20:03.287585610Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:14:16.904103570Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:14:16.904103570Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18.3","title":"Task: Analytics dashboard view (KPI tiles + sparklines + top movers)","description":"## Goal
Implement the default Analytics dashboard view: a dense, stylish “KPI wall” that answers the most common questions in <5 seconds.

## Layout (80x24 friendly)
- Header: filter ribbon (time range, agent/workspace/source).
- Body: grid of KPI tiles (2-3 columns depending on width).
- Right/bottom: ‘Top movers’ list (agents/workspaces) with deltas vs prior period.
- Footer: key hints.

## KPIs (v1)
All KPIs should be computed from rollups via `crate::analytics` (z9fse.12):
- Tokens today / last 7d / last 30d:
  - show both `api_tokens_total` and `content_tokens_est_total` where useful
  - show coverage % to explain discrepancies
- Burn rate:
  - tokens/hour over last 2h (and trailing 24h average)
- Tool intensity:
  - tool calls per 1k tokens (and absolute tool calls)
- Planning:
  - plan message count + plan token share
- Cost tiles:
  - if cost is available (z9fse.10), show USD today / 30d; otherwise show “cost not available” with hint.

Each KPI tile should include a mini sparkline for trend over the selected range.

## UX
- `Enter` on a KPI tile jumps to Explorer pre-configured to that metric.
- `Enter` on a top-mover row filters Explorer to that agent/workspace.

## Performance
- Dashboard queries should be O(#buckets) and cached for the current filter set.
- Prefer a single multi-query pass per refresh tick rather than dozens of tiny queries.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dashboard renders in < 16ms on typical terminals (no jank).
- All KPIs have sensible fallback when a metric is missing.
- Drilldown interactions work and preserve global filters.

## Testing & Logging
- Snapshot tests for 80x24 and 120x40.
- Unit tests for KPI computation glue code (mostly in analytics query layer).
- PTY e2e: open dashboard, select KPI, drilldown, exit.
- Logging: per-view query timing summary (rows scanned, cache hits).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:14:46.577428877Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T06:02:04.879489667Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T06:02:04.879466363Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","ftui","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:14:46.577428877Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:14:46.577428877Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:14:46.577428877Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:14:46.577428877Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.8","title":"Task: Adaptive rendering/perf budgets (degradation levels, auto-disable animation, diff strategy tuning)","description":"## Goal
Use ftui's degradation + evidence/telemetry systems to keep cass smooth and flicker-free under load.

## Features
- Degradation levels:
  - full styling
  - reduced decorative
  - essential-only
- Auto-disable animations when FPS drops below threshold.
- Diff strategy tuning to minimize terminal I/O.

## Implementation Notes
- Use `Frame.degradation` hints and widgets' essential/decorative split.
- Consider `ftui_runtime::RuntimeDiffConfig` and diff strategy selection.

## Tests
- Harness tests:
  - flicker detection
  - determinism under repeated renders
- Benchmark that measures diff size and render time on large result sets.
